votes.rationale: The selection/scoring path behaved correctly on the tested 2-candidate Librarian panel across three behavior checks: score recompute, select dry-run, and force-winner. Code inspection of panelSelectCore.mjs found no fixed-shape assumptions. The shared implementation appears shape-agnostic, supporting reasonable inference that other panels sharing the same code path will behave consistently.

The proof target was correctly bounded to selection/scoring behavior on the current 2-candidate shape. Zero-score testing is sufficient for a shape-parity proof since the scoring math is deterministic and count-independent. The motion is honest about remaining gaps: differentiated-score ranking, selector activation, and broader runtime behavior remain unproven and are appropriate targets for future motions.

The behavior-proof layer is now validated on the tested surface. Evidence is calibrated: one panel runtime-tested, shared selection core inspected, three distinct behavior paths exercised. The motion correctly avoids overclaiming equivalence across all panels or candidate counts. The Phase 1 proof chain from canon through behavior is internally consistent.
